# terrible_community_sap_libs_sap_snote (Resource)





<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `conn_password` (String) The required password for the SAP system.
- `conn_username` (String) The required username for the SAP system.
- `host` (String) The required host for the SAP system. Can be either an FQDN or IP Address.
- `host_id` (String) ID of the `terrible_host` to run this task against

### Optional

- `async_seconds` (Number) Run the task asynchronously, timing out after this many seconds. 0 = synchronous (default).
- `changed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered changed (e.g. 'false').
- `client` (String) The client number to connect to. You must quote the value to ensure retaining the leading zeros.
- `delegate_to_id` (String) ID of another terrible_host to delegate execution to.
- `environment` (String) Environment variables set for the task (dict of nameâ†’value).
- `failed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered failed.
- `ignore_errors` (Boolean) When true, a failed task does not raise a Terraform error.
- `poll_interval` (Number) Polling interval in seconds when async_seconds > 0. Defaults to 15.
- `skip_tags` (String) Skip tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `snote` (String) With the `snote` paramter only implementation and deimplementation will work. Upload SNOTES to the System is only available if `snote_path` is provided.
- `snote_path` (String) The path to the extracted SNOTE txt file. The File could be extracted from SAR package. If `snote_path` is not provided, the `snote` parameter must be defined. The SNOTE txt file must be at a place where the SAP System is authorized for. For example `/usr/sap/trans/files`.
- `state` (String) The decision what to do with the SNOTE. Could be `'present'`, `'absent'`
- `sysnr` (String) The system number of the SAP system. You must quote the value to ensure retaining the leading zeros.
- `tags` (String) Run only tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `timeout` (Number) Override the default execution timeout (seconds). Defaults to 300.
- `triggers` (String) Arbitrary map of values; any change triggers task re-execution

### Read-Only

- `changed` (Boolean) Whether the task reported a change
- `id` (String) Unique task resource ID
- `msg` (String) A small execution description.
- `out` (String) A complete description of the SNOTE implementation. If this is available.
